raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "prescriptive definition of divine service as ethical-psychological purification",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "a divine or transcendent reality exists to which human conduct can constitute 'service'",
    "violent passion, evil affection, rashness, vanity, and discontent constitute defilements that obstruct proper relation to that reality",
    "purity from these states is both attainable and sufficient for fulfilling the relation",
    "the gods (or God) are themselves free from or opposed to such states"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally coherent within a Stoic-theistic framework that equates virtue with alignment to cosmic or divine order",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "a transcendent order exists whose requirements are met by sustained emotional discipline, or the adopted discipline produces measurable psychological or ethical benefits",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "the posited divine service actually demands different conduct, or systematic suppression of affect causes psychological harm or distorts accurate perception of reality"
}
